inspired_by: Hermes Agent v0.12 Curator: rubric-based review, active-update bias, scoped memory/skills toolsets, usage ranking, pinned skills, reversible archive.

importance_levels.critical: Core harness and safety skills. Never archive automatically.
importance_levels.important: Rarely used but high-value skills. Warn only; never auto-archive.
importance_levels.normal: Standard skills. Eligible for stale review and reversible archive.
importance_levels.low: One-off or experimental skills. First candidates for archive after review.

new_skill_gate: Search nearest existing skills and references first.
Reject if the lesson fits as a paragraph, reference, script, or eval in an existing skill.
Reject if the trigger would fire too often or duplicates another skill.
Require verification evidence that the new skill improves output quality, reliability, safety, token cost, or repeatability.
Keep the new SKILL.md compact and move heavy detail into references or scripts.
promotion_gate: No raw secrets, private data, prompt injection, or unverified model claims.
Every promoted memory or skill patch needs source, scope, date, expiry, and verification.
Skill edits are small, reversible, and catalog/audit checked.
Archived skills remain on disk and can be restored.
